title: Unweighted and Dijkstra shortest paths

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Unweighted and Dijkstra shortest paths; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Unweighted and Dijkstra shortest paths
sources.claim: Shortest-path algorithm choice depends on edge weights: BFS for unweighted, Dijkstra for nonnegative weights, and Bellman-Ford when negative edges may occur.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Shortest-path algorithm choice depends on edge weights: BFS for unweighted, Dijkstra for nonnegative weights, and Bellman-Ford when negative edges may occur.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Dijkstra finalizes the smallest tentative distance only under nonnegative edge weights. Your goal is to select, implement and test a shortest-path algorithm while stating invalid weight assumptions. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Graph representation and traversal; Priority queue applications; Breadth-first and depth-first search. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Shortest-path algorithm choice depends on edge weights: BFS for unweighted, Dijkstra for nonnegative weights, and Bellman-Ford when negative edges may occur. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Negative cycles can make distances undefined, overflow needs care, and heuristics change guarantees. The draft therefore rejects: Dijkstra remains correct with negative edges as long as there is no negative cycle.

includes: Use BFS for unit edges and Dijkstra for nonnegative weights with priority updates and predecessor reconstruction.
Select, implement and test a shortest-path algorithm while stating invalid weight assumptions.

In C, represent adjacency and disjoint sets with explicit buffers and ownership; C++ uses vector-based lists and standard priority structures; Java uses typed collections and careful object equality; Python uses list/dict/set and heapq, with the same graph or partition invariant and complexity target.
excludes: Negative cycles can make distances undefined, overflow needs care, and heuristics change guarantees.

outcomes: Select, implement and test a shortest-path algorithm while stating invalid weight assumptions.
